nodes.label: Agent A — Scanner
nodes.rationale: Slow path (1-3s) for 'should we buy this?' — fully LLM-driven, returns Decision with full FullAnalysis. Chosen because speed is not the primary constraint when evaluating whether to buy; thoroughness and auditability matter more.

nodes.label: Agent B — Sniper
nodes.rationale: Fast path for 'BUY NOW or skip.' Four decision modes (rule, llm, hybrid, hybrid_audit) chosen to let operators trade off latency vs. AI reasoning depth depending on token age and execution urgency.

nodes.label: hybrid_audit Decision Mode
nodes.rationale: Rule path decides instantly (<1 ms), then background coroutine runs LLM to second-opinion that decision. Bridges speed and AI quality without LLM in the hot path. Disagreements written to DecisionLog for offline tuning.

nodes.label: KnowledgePack
nodes.rationale: Passive context the LLM reads — markdown rules and JSON data files prepended to the system prompt once per run. Zero cost beyond token usage. Chosen over dynamic tool calls for facts that should be known upfront.

nodes.label: LLMRouter
nodes.rationale: Named LLMRouter (not ZetrynRouter) because it routes LLM calls generically, not Zetryn-specific state. Multi-provider failover with per-model RPM/RPD/TPM/TPD sliding window throttle to avoid reactive 429 handling.

nodes.label: ReflectiveNode
nodes.rationale: Rule-based (no LLM) — cheap and deterministic, safe for live loops. Buckets losing decisions by feature, writes lessons_text into state.scratch for prompt injection. Closes the learning loop without adding LLM cost.

nodes.label: safety_gate
nodes.rationale: Hard gate that instantly rejects obvious junk (honeypot, mint authority active, dev rug history, bundle attacks, dead liquidity). Sub-millisecond, no LLM call burned on rugs. Keeps AI analysis focused on real candidates.

nodes.label: Guardrail
nodes.rationale: Sanity-checks LLM output. Can demote a verdict but never promote — hard reality (liquidity floor, sniper density) always wins over a hallucinated bullish call. Prevents AI overconfidence from driving bad trades.

nodes.label: Graph Engine (State, Node, Edge, Graph, Command)
nodes.norm_label: graph engine (state, node, edge, graph, command)

nodes.label: Push Data Ingress Pattern
nodes.rationale: Recommended for production: bot fetches data, builds TokenInput, calls agent.run(). Latency is predictable and no surprise external calls occur inside the agent graph.

nodes.label: Pull Data Ingress Pattern
nodes.rationale: Implement DataProvider protocol; agent calls provider.fetch(mint). Useful for backtests with HistoricalDataProvider. Same graph runs test, backtest, and live — only the provider changes.

nodes.label: Knowledge vs Skills Distinction
nodes.rationale: Knowledge = passive context LLM reads (free, prepended once). Skills = callable functions LLM invokes (real API/RPC hit per call). Rule of thumb: if the LLM should know something upfront, it is knowledge; if it should do something to get an answer, it is a skill.

nodes.label: Three-Phase LLM Evolution
nodes.rationale: Phase 1: public free providers (Groq/Gemini/OpenRouter). Phase 2: paid providers with optional parallel specialist nodes. Phase 3: Zetryn fine-tuned models (Easfus/Medifus/Hardes) bundled in subscription. Code structure does not change across phases — only ProviderConfig does.

nodes.label: Strict Dependency Rule
nodes.rationale: zetryn/ imports nothing from trading/ or strategies/. trading/ imports nothing. strategies/ imports both. Enables users to import zetryn/ in their own bot with custom trading-shaped schemas for non-Solana domains.

nodes.label: AI-First Design Philosophy
nodes.rationale: LLM as primary analyst rather than threshold/formula-based heuristics. Motivation: rug techniques evolve, qualitative signals (Twitter velocity, KOL patterns, bonding curve regime) outpace hardcoded formulas. Tradeoff: LLM cost managed by hard gates that pre-filter obvious junk before any LLM call.

nodes.label: RouterEntry
nodes.rationale: Represents a (provider, model) pair — not a provider alone. Two models from the same provider are two separate entries with their own KeyPool. A 429 on one model should not automatically penalise the other model from the same provider.

nodes.label: Scanner Graph Shape with Reflect Node
nodes.norm_label: scanner graph shape with reflect node

nodes.label: Analyst System Prompt Layering
nodes.rationale: Layering order (top to bottom): KnowledgePack system blocks (static rules), reflection lessons (dynamic from past outcomes), analyst persona + per-token fact sheet. Static rules provide stable guardrails; dynamic lessons adapt to recent performance.

nodes.label: Reliability Pattern (LLMRouter Production Default)
nodes.rationale: Free-tier providers spike under rate-limit pressure (single Groq p95 ~11s vs 1.5s median). Recommended fix: LLMRouter with >=2 providers (Groq + Gemini Flash). Preventive sliding-window throttle skips throttled entries without a network call — failover is local and free, not reactive on 429.

nodes.label: CAPABILITIES.md — Single Source of Truth
nodes.rationale: Designated as the single source of truth for project status. README points here; architecture decision records point here for milestone status; CHANGELOG references same versions. Chosen to avoid status drift across multiple docs.
